# Hub21 Agent Profile API

Real estate agent, loan originator, and staff profile data for Century 21 Realty Masters and Full Realty Services. Backed by WordPress + BuddyPress on a multisite at https://myhub21.com. Data flows from a Google Sheet (canonical roster) + Courted (RE production stats) + Modex (LO production stats CSV) + base.frs (legacy) into WP user_meta + BP xprofile fields, then served via BP REST API.

This file is written for LLM agents (Claude, GPT, etc.) that need to query, interpret, or write profile data. It explains naming conventions, available endpoints, member-type taxonomy, and the sync architecture so an agent can safely reason about the system without re-deriving it from scratch.

## Identity and access

- **Who can read**: any authenticated WordPress user via the BuddyPress REST API. Unauthenticated requests receive limited public fields only.
- **Auth (preferred)**: WordPress Application Passwords. Send `Authorization: Basic <base64(user:appPwd)>` on every request. Issue app passwords from the user's WP profile page or via WP-CLI: `wp user application-password create <user> "agent-name"`.
- **Auth (browser)**: standard WP login cookies + nonce. Used by in-portal JS.
- **BP REST base**: `https://myhub21.com/wp-json/buddypress/v1/`
- **WP REST base**: `https://myhub21.com/wp-json/wp/v2/` (for user_meta passthrough on app-password-authorized clients)

### Member sites in the multisite

- `https://myhub21.com/` ŌĆö root site, hosts the primary member directory and all `sales_associate`, `staff`, and `commercial_agent` members.
- `https://myhub21.com/lending/` ŌĆö sub-site for `loan_originator` members. Same REST schema; cross-network queries should hit the root site, which has the full network user list.

## Field naming policy (read this before writing any code)

Naming follows a strict priority order so client code stays readable and portable:

1. **Native WP/BP keys take precedence.** Use `description` not `bio`; `nickname` not `preferred_name`; `facebook` not `fb_url`. If WordPress core or BuddyPress defines a field, that name wins.
2. **Industry-standard names for the rest.** `ltm_sales_volume`, `mls_id`, `nmls`, `dre_license`, `license_state`. These match how the broader real-estate/mortgage tooling ecosystem names things.
3. **No `frs_` prefix anywhere in new code.** Legacy keys like `frs_company_role`, `frs_nmls`, `frs_legacy_uuid` still exist on prod for backwards compatibility and are dual-written by `MetaKeyAlias`. New integrations must read and write the canonical (un-prefixed) name only.
4. **Vendor names only as link-back metadata.** External system IDs use the `<vendor>_id` form: `courted_id`, `modex_id`, `twenty_crm_id`, `aad_object_id`. These are stored under `ids.*` in the canonical response shape.

## Member type slugs

BuddyPress member types are the primary taxonomy for role/function:

- `sales_associate` ŌĆö real estate agent (sells residential or commercial property)
- `loan_originator` ŌĆö mortgage loan officer (NMLS-licensed, originates loans)
- `staff` ŌĆö non-producing org members: admin, marketing, operations, ownership
- `commercial_agent` ŌĆö RE agent with commercial specialization tag (often also has `sales_associate`)

A single user can hold **multiple** member types. Assignment uses the BP API:

```
bp_set_member_type( $user_id, 'sales_associate', true );  // true = append, don't replace
bp_set_member_type( $user_id, 'commercial_agent', true );
```

In the SQLite warehouse (`data/agents.db`), the multi-type assignment lives in a JSON list on the `member_types` column. Read it as JSON, not a single string.

## Endpoint cheat sheet

All paths are relative to `https://myhub21.com/wp-json/`.

### Members

- **`GET buddypress/v1/members`** ŌĆö list members.
  - Filters: `member_type=sales_associate`, `search=tolu`, `xprofile[<field_id>]=<value>`, `page`, `per_page` (max 100), `include[]=<user_id>`, `exclude[]=<user_id>`.
  - Response shape: `[{ id, name, user_login, avatar_urls, xprofile: { ... }, member_types: [ ... ], ... }, ...]`
- **`GET buddypress/v1/members/{id}`** ŌĆö single member with full xprofile payload.
- **`GET buddypress/v1/members/me`** ŌĆö currently authenticated member. Useful for "who am I" checks before mutating profile data.

### xprofile (extended profile fields)

- **`GET buddypress/v1/xprofile/groups`** ŌĆö field group definitions, organized by category: Personal & Contact, Bio, RE Production, LO Production, Credentials, Employment, Social. Use this to discover field IDs before querying values.
- **`GET buddypress/v1/xprofile/groups/{group_id}?fetch_fields=true`** ŌĆö group with its child field definitions, including `id`, `name`, `type`, and `options`.
- **`GET buddypress/v1/xprofile/data/{field_id}/{user_id}`** ŌĆö single field value for a single user. Cheapest read when you already know the field ID.
- **`POST buddypress/v1/xprofile/data/{field_id}/{user_id}`** ŌĆö write a value (requires the authenticated user to be the profile owner or have `edit_users`). Body: `{ "value": "..." }`.

### Groups (offices and regions)

Offices and regions are BuddyPress groups, distinguished by `group_type`:

- **`GET buddypress/v1/groups?group_type=office`** ŌĆö list all offices (e.g., Walnut, Diamond Bar, Anaheim Hills).
- **`GET buddypress/v1/groups?group_type=region`** ŌĆö list parent regions (e.g., Bay Area, Inland Empire). Each office links to a region via a `parent_id` xprofile field on the group.
- **`GET buddypress/v1/groups/{id}/members`** ŌĆö members in a given office or region. Supports `per_page` and `page`.

## Field categories

Fields are grouped semantically. Below are the categories an LLM should understand when reading or writing profile data.

- **Identity** ŌĆö WP-native user fields: `user_login`, `user_email`, `display_name`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `nickname`, `description` (BP bio), `user_url`. Stored in `wp_users` + `wp_usermeta`.
- **Contact** ŌĆö phones, alternate emails, social URLs: `phone`, `mobile`, `business_email`, `facebook`, `instagram`, `linkedin`, `twitter`, `youtube`, `tiktok`.
- **Credentials** ŌĆö license and registry info: `nmls`, `dre_license`, `license_state`, `license_type`, `mls_id`.
- **Employment** ŌĆö org affiliation: `office`, `region`, `brand` (e.g., "Century 21 Masters"), `job_title`, `department`, `status` (active/inactive/archived).
- **RE Production** ŌĆö `sales_associate` only: `ltm_sales_volume`, `ltm_transactions`, `active_listings`, `closed_listings_ytd`. Sourced from Courted.
- **LO Production** ŌĆö `loan_originator` only: `ltm_loan_volume`, `ltm_loan_units`, `loans_in_pipeline`. Sourced from Modex CSV.
- **Vendor metadata** ŌĆö external service link-backs under `ids.*`: `courted_id`, `modex_id`, `twenty_crm_id`, `aad_object_id` (Entra/Azure AD), `legacy_uuid`, `legacy_id`.

## Sync architecture (data flow)

Understanding the flow prevents accidentally clobbering canonical data.

### Sources

- **Google Sheet** ŌĆö canonical roster. Single source of truth for who exists, their employment, contact info, and member type assignments. Pulled nightly.
- **Courted** ŌĆö RE production stats per agent. Pulled per-agent via Courted API, matched on email or `courted_id`.
- **Modex CSV** ŌĆö LO production stats. Bulk import once per cycle, matched on NMLS.
- **base.frs** ŌĆö legacy MySQL system. Being retired. Read-only mirror exists for backfill of historical UUIDs.

### Pipeline

1. Sources are normalized into the local SQLite warehouse at `data/agents.db`.
2. `LocalAggregateSync` (a WP-CLI command, lives in `frs-wp-users` plugin) pushes SQLite rows to WordPress production. **Append-only** ŌĆö it never overwrites identity fields on users that have an `aadObjectId` (those are Entra-managed).
3. On the WordPress side, `MetaKeyAlias` dual-writes canonical keys to their legacy `frs_*` counterparts so old plugins keep working without modification.
4. `BuddyPressXProfileSchema` auto-mirrors `user_meta` to BP `xprofile` fields bidirectionally, with a re-entry guard to prevent infinite loops. This is what makes a `user_meta` write visible via the BP REST API.

### Diagram

```
Google Sheet  ŌöĆŌöÉ
Courted API   ŌöĆŌöżŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢║ SQLite (agents.db) ŌöĆŌöĆ[LocalAggregateSync WP-CLI]ŌöĆŌöĆŌ¢║ WP prod
Modex CSV     ŌöĆŌöż                                                          Ōöé
base.frs      ŌöĆŌöś                                                          Ō¢╝
                                                              user_meta ŌŚäŌöĆŌ¢║ xprofile
                                                                          via BuddyPressXProfileSchema
                                                                          + MetaKeyAlias (legacy mirror)
                                                                          Ōöé
                                                                          Ō¢╝
                                                                    BP REST API
```

## Things NOT to do (LLM guardrails)

These are the most common mistakes when an agent first works with this system. Avoid them.

- **Don't write to user_meta keys with the `frs_` prefix.** Write to the canonical name. The `MetaKeyAlias` hook handles the legacy mirror for you. Writing both sides yourself causes race conditions and double-writes.
- **Don't overwrite identity fields (`user_email`, `display_name`, `first_name`, `last_name`) on users with `aadObjectId` set.** Those users are Microsoft Entra-managed; their identity must round-trip from Azure AD. Sync code already skips them ŌĆö manual writes must too.
- **Don't set `frs_company_role`.** It's a dead cache of `bp_member_type` left over from a prior refactor. Use `bp_get_member_type( $user_id )` to read role, and `bp_set_member_type()` to write. The cache column was dropped from SQLite as of 2026-05-16.
- **Don't query `frs_legacy_uuid` or `frs_legacy_id` for new features.** The base.frs system is being retired. These keys are kept only for one-off historical lookups. New code should treat the WP `user_id` as the canonical identifier.
- **Don't bypass `BuddyPressXProfileSchema` by writing directly to `wp_bp_xprofile_data`.** Always write to user_meta; the mirror hook propagates the change. Direct xprofile writes skip the dual-write and leave user_meta stale.
- **Don't assume one user has one member type.** A commercial agent often holds both `sales_associate` and `commercial_agent`. A team lead may hold `staff` and `loan_originator`. Always iterate `bp_get_member_type( $user_id, false )` (the `false` returns the full array).
- **Don't reset passwords.** Users authenticate via WPO365/Microsoft SSO. Resetting a WP password silently breaks future SSO flows for that account; if access is broken, send the WP lost-password link or fix the Entra account upstream.

## Related artifacts

- [OpenAPI spec](./openapi.yaml) ŌĆö formal machine-readable definition of every endpoint, parameter, and response schema. Load this into a code generator or schema-aware client.
- [Swagger UI](./swagger-ui.html) ŌĆö interactive in-browser docs page backed by the OpenAPI spec. Useful for humans poking at endpoints.
- [Field map](./api-field-map.md) ŌĆö original schema/WRITE/READ map showing canonical names, the WordPress location each is persisted to, and the resolver priority for legacy aliases.
- [Sync code (LocalAggregateSync)](https://github.com/fullrealtyservices/frs-wp-users/blob/main/includes/Integrations/LocalAggregateSync.php) ŌĆö the WP-CLI sync class that pushes SQLite ŌåÆ prod.
- [MetaKeyAlias](https://github.com/fullrealtyservices/frs-wp-users/blob/main/includes/Integrations/MetaKeyAlias.php) ŌĆö the dual-write hook for legacy `frs_*` keys.
- [BuddyPressXProfileSchema](https://github.com/fullrealtyservices/frs-wp-users/blob/main/includes/Integrations/BuddyPressXProfileSchema.php) ŌĆö the bidirectional user_meta Ōåö xprofile mirror.

## Quick reference: a complete read example

Fetch a single member with all profile fields, using app-password auth:

```
curl -u "derintolu:xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx" \
  "https://myhub21.com/wp-json/buddypress/v1/members/2?populate_extras=true"
```

Fetch all loan originators in one office:

```
curl -u "derintolu:xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx" \
  "https://myhub21.com/wp-json/buddypress/v1/members?member_type=loan_originator&xprofile[123]=Walnut&per_page=100"
```

(Replace `123` with the actual `office` xprofile field ID from `GET xprofile/groups`.)
